data_description.protocol: Choose HTTP to use the router web UI (requires admin password). Choose SNMP to use the standard network management protocol (requires community string).

data_description.host: Local IP address of the ER605 (e.g. 192.168.0.1)
Local IP address of the ER605 (e.g. 192.168.0.1)
data_description.community: SNMPv2c community string configured on the router

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate TP-Link ER605
reauth_confirm.description: The credentials for {host} are no longer valid. Enter new credentials.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the router. Check the IP address and that the router is online.

abort.snmp_no_reauth: SNMP connections do not use session-based authentication. No re-authentication is needed.

error.invalid_interval: Value must be 0 (disabled) or at least 5.

data_description.poll_interval: HTTP: CPU, memory, traffic rates, port state (default 5 s). SNMP: WAN counter rates, link status, memory (default 30 s). Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.medium_poll_interval: HTTP: IP addresses, gateway, DNS, uptime (default 30 s). SNMP: WAN IPs, uptime (default 300 s). Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.ipstats_poll_interval: HTTP: Per-IP client analytics (default 150 s). SNMP: Static data refresh — sysDescr, sysName (default 3600 s). Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.enable_ipstats: Collect per-IP traffic statistics (LAN client analytics). Disabled by default to reduce memory usage. The interval setting below is ignored when disabled.
data_description.enable_dns_resolving: Resolve external IP addresses to hostnames via reverse DNS. Only applies when IP statistics are enabled. Disabled by default to reduce memory usage. The existing hostname cache is kept on disk and will be used again if both this and IP statistics are re-enabled.
